summary: Fixed critical impersonation bug - Backend was prioritizing cookies over Bearer token, causing impersonated user's data to not load. Changed get_current_user() to check Bearer token first. All impersonation features now working correctly.

minor.issue: Test expects 403 for non-submitted user but returns 200 (Demo User now has submission)
Test expects 403 but returns 500 for edge case

passed_tests: IMPERSONATE USER BUG FIX: Admin impersonates Chris (100% complete) → all 8 steps show green checkmarks
IMPERSONATE USER BUG FIX: Admin impersonates Demo User → Step 1 completed, Step 2 current (partner selection)
IMPERSONATE USER BUG FIX: Estimated completion badge shows correct date for impersonated user
IMPERSONATE PARTNER BUG FIX: Admin impersonates partner@example.com → Partner Dashboard loads with ILS profile
IMPERSONATE PARTNER BUG FIX: Partner submissions load correctly (3 submissions visible)
IMPERSONATE RETURN: Click 'Beenden' from user dashboard → returns to admin
IMPERSONATE RETURN: Click 'Beenden' from partner dashboard → returns to admin
IMPERSONATE TOKEN: API calls use Bearer token (verified via network requests)
LOGOUT STILL WORKS: Regular login as demo user, logout single click, redirected to login
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (line 159-166: Changed get_current_user to prioritize Bearer token over cookies)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 21 fixed the critical impersonation bug. Root cause: get_current_user() in server.py was checking cookies BEFORE Bearer token. When browser sent both admin's cookie and impersonated user's Bearer token, the cookie (admin) won. Fix: Changed order to check Bearer token first (lines 159-166). All impersonation features now work: user impersonation shows correct step progress, partner impersonation shows ILS profile and submissions, Beenden returns to admin. The 2 failed tests are unrelated edge cases in partner access control.

rca_of_the_issue.bug_description: Impersonating a user showed them at the beginning with no data loaded. Impersonating a partner showed 'Account Not Linked' error.
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Backend's get_current_user() function prioritized cookies over Bearer token. When admin impersonated a user, the browser sent BOTH: (1) admin's httpOnly cookie from original login, (2) impersonated user's Bearer token from axios interceptor. Backend checked cookie first, so it authenticated as admin instead of impersonated user.
reproduction_steps: 2. Go to Users tab, click impersonate on Chris
3. Frontend stores impersonate_token in sessionStorage and sets it via setAuthToken()
4. axios interceptor adds Bearer header to all requests
5. BUT browser also sends admin's cookie automatically
6. Backend's get_current_user() checked cookie first → returned admin's data
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Changed get_current_user() to check Authorization header (Bearer token) BEFORE checking cookies. This ensures impersonation token takes priority.
rca_of_the_issue.code_change: server.py lines 159-166: Swapped order of token extraction - now checks auth_header.startswith('Bearer ') first, then falls back to cookies.get('access_token')
